title: Unexpected output from `quay.io/libpod/banner` in Basic Networking Guide for Podman tutorial
body:
/kind bug **Description** I receive unexpected output when carrying out the steps of the tutorial [Basic Networking Guide for Podman](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/basic_networking.md). **Describe the results you received:** ``` $ curl http://127.0.0.1:8080 404 Not Found

404 Not Found

nginx
``` And same for `curl http://127.0.0.1:8081`. **Describe the results you expected:** ``` $ curl http://127.0.0.1:8080 ___ __ / _ \___ ___/ /_ _ ___ ____ / ___/ _ \/ _ / ' \/ _ `/ _ \ /_/ \___/\_,_/_/_/_/\_,_/_//_/ ``` And same for `curl http://127.0.0.1:8081`. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** It's probably a problem with the image `quay.io/libpod/banner`. (Where is the source code for the image located?) **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 3.4.4 API Version: 3.4.4 Go Version: go1.16.8 Built: Wed Dec 8 11:45:07 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.23.1 cgroupControllers: - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.1.0-2.fc35.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.0, commit: ' cpus: 8 distribution: distribution: fedora variant: workstation version: "35" eventLogger: journald hostname: nuc idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 kernel: 5.16.16-200.fc35.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 17559465984 memTotal: 33536753664 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.4.3-1.fc35.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.4.3 commit: 61c9600d1335127eba65632731e2d72bc3f0b9e8 spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: true seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: true serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: slirp4netns-1.1.12-2.fc35.x86_64 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.1.12 commit: 7a104a101aa3278a2152351a082a6df71f57c9a3 libslirp: 4.6.1 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3 libseccomp: 2.5.3 swapFree: 8589930496 swapTotal: 8589930496 uptime: 54h 10m 4.93s (Approximately 2.25 days) plugins: log: - k8s-file - none - journald network: - bridge - macvlan volume: - local registries: search: - registry.fedoraproject.org - registry.access.redhat.com - docker.io - quay.io store: configFile: /home/robin/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 1 paused: 0 running: 1 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home/robin/.local/share/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: btrfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageStore: number: 1 runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers volumePath: /home/robin/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.4.4 Built: 1638999907 BuiltTime: Wed Dec 8 11:45:07 2021 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.16.8 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.4.4 ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` podman-3.4.4-1.fc35.x86_64 ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)** No **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** bare metal

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Could you try this on podman 4.0?

I got the same result using `podman 4.0.2` (I used the latest pre-release iso of Fedora 36 running in a Gnome Boxes VM.)

I'm pretty sure the problem is that the `quay.io/libpod/banner` image has been broken for the past 6 months. It does not print a banner. It only prints a 404 error page.

I got further through this tutorial when I switched from `quay.io/libpod/banner` to the Nginx Docker image discussed in Podman's [Introduction](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/Introduction.html). (except that image does not include `curl` so I couldn't perform the last step about _"From another rootless container..."_)

I too ran into this today while I was working with Podman + netavark and needed a quick-n-dirty http response server.

The problem within the container is that the file:  `/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf` should have been placed in `/etc/nginx/http.d/default.conf`.  The existing `http.d/default.conf` is hardwired to only return a 404 result.

Once I moved it (`conf.d/default.conf`) and invoked:  `nginx -s reload`, the banner worked correctly.

HTH.

@rhatdan That I do not know, I troubleshooted this directly on running container via `podman exec -it banner /bin/sh`

I went ahead and did a `commit` and `push` up to: https://quay.io/repository/terratech/banner
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@baude did you ever get to this?
